executive_summary:
Your supply chain is amber primarily due to geopolitical clustering: four critical-to-high exposure suppliers (Huizhou BYD, Smoore, EVE Energy, Texas Instruments) are either China-based or Taiwan-exposed, facing compounding US-China trade friction and export controls that could cascade through EMS and battery categories. Separately, three printing substrate suppliers (Stora Enso, IP Sun, Sappi) are geopolitically isolated but in different theatres—Finland's NATO exposure, China's tech controls, and South Africa's energy crisis—so they're less likely to cascade but each needs individual contingency planning.

The urgent move is a sourcing audit of the China cluster (especially Huizhou BYD and Smoore, both critical BAT exposure) to identify secondary suppliers or nearshoring options before Q2 trade policy tightens; Texas Instruments' unexplained -3.1% stock drop despite high BAT exposure warrants a direct earnings call or investor note review to rule out supply-side signals. Sappi and South Africa's energy instability is worth monitoring but lower urgency unless you're seeing production delays already. The macro dashboard gaps (US and China economic data still placeholder) are limiting visibility—prioritize closing those within one sprint.
